en: Employees must be informed of the locations and proper use of firefighting equipment. In workplaces with a required or appointed fire safety officer or fire brigade, employees working in risk areas must receive instruction in the use of fire extinguishers. Fire safety wardens must be appointed where required and must have completed relevant training of at least six hours. On construction sites, a sufficient number of employees must be familiar with the use of fire extinguishers.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 14(4), 43(2,6), 45(6)\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 45(7)

Fire safety officers must be appointed when required by authority. Their responsibilities must include employee fire behavior information, fire self-monitoring, incipient fire response, workplace evacuation, and preparation for fire brigade operations.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 43(1,3)

Fire prevention precautions must be established to protect employee life and health, including appropriate measures to prevent, control, and respond to fire hazards in the workplace.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 25(1)

Fire alarm and evacuation drills must occur annually, with repeats after deficiencies, in workplaces with a required or appointed fire safety officer or brigade. On a construction site, annual fire drills must occur where fire alarm systems are required and records maintained.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 45(5)\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 46(3)
Designated persons familiar with fire extinguishers and evacuation procedures must be appointed.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 44a(1)\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 25(4)

Suitable and sufficient firefighting aids based on fire classes, fire load, use, and size must be provided, such as water, fire blankets, fire sand, wall hydrants, portable fire extinguishers, or wheeled fire extinguishers. The visibility and accessibility of fire extinguishing equipment must be ensured with clear markings.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 42(1,4)\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 20(7), 45(1-2), 45(6)
Fire extinguishers and fixed extinguishing systems must be inspected at least every two years.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 13(2)

Inspection records must be retained for at least three years. A fire safety logbook must record checks, deficiencies, drills, and fires in workplaces with a required or appointed fire safety officer or brigade.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 13(5), 45(3)
